title: Has the Shortened Drug Distribution Chain Cut Drug Prices? Evidence from China

notes: EVENT STUDY REVIEWED — 3 custom figures created (figure1_replication, figure2_harmonized, figure3_cs_pairbalanced). All-eventually-treated. Li et al. (2026). Table 3 Col 3 (TWFE with controls). Stata: reghdfe lnprice treat1 GDPadj old POP NumHos NumPhy NumBed GovExp inten InsurExp_Urban, absorb(yearmonth id) cluster(clusterid). ALL 13 cohorts eventually treated → CS-NYT only. Stata CS: csdid lnprice, i(id) t(yearmonth) g(policyyearmonth1) cluster(clusternum) agg(simple) → 0.012. TWFE clusters at clusterid, CS at clusternum (Pattern 30). Monthly data (yearmonth as %tm 660-719). BJS: did_imputation with same controls, cluster(clusternum). | AUDIT 2026-04-19: Paper-auditor WITHIN_TOLERANCE (2.44% rounding, paper 0.019 → ours 0.01854). LESSON 10 SWEEP SUCCESS: allow_unbalanced=true previously caused CS-NYT sign reversal (-0.0065 vs paper +0.012). Sweep 2026-04-19 flipped to allow_unbalanced=false — CS-NYT simple now +0.034 (sign matches paper, magnitude +79% vs TWFE). Skeptic FAIL verdict on CS-DID is stale (pre-sweep). 11 pre-period coefs all significant is PAPER DESIGN FRAGILITY (all-eventually-treated with forbidden comparisons), not implementation. HonestDiD break at Mbar=0.75 follows from pre-trend magnitude. 11th Lesson 7 direct-level collapse: Spec A (CS-NYT+9 ctrls staggered all-eventually-treated) → 0/NA. LOW rating now reflects design fragility (pre-trends), not implementation bug (fixed).
